title: Apply for the Pathways Employability Programme
description: Apply for the 2026 Pathways Employability Programme – a Ministry of Youth scheme that helps young people get ready for work.
summary: Pathways is the Ministry of Youth’s employability programme for young Barbadians who are not in school, training or full-time work. It combines classroom training, a work placement and one-to-one mentoring.

The programme is designed for people who are still finding their direction. You do not need formal qualifications to apply. The training covers basic employability skills – communication, team work, time-keeping, customer service – plus the specific skills needed for the area of work you choose.

The work placement is usually with a Barbadian employer. The Ministry matches participants and employers based on the skills the participant has been training in and the type of work the employer offers.
There is no fee for the programme. Most participants come out with a clearer sense of what they want to do and the contacts to help them get there.
who_can_apply: people who are not in school, training or full-time work

items: your full name, address and date of birth
your email address and phone number
the type of training or work you are interested in

items.name: Most recent school report or certificates

how_long: You will get a reply within 4 to 6 weeks of the closing date.

options.heading: Online
content: Complete the Pathways Employability Programme: Application Form (2026) on the Ministry website.

contact.agency: Ministry of Youth, Sports and Community Empowerment
contact.address:
Sky Mall, Haggatt Hall
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Aged 17 to 30’ is the commonly-quoted range for Pathways. The form may use different wording.
Description of what the programme covers (classroom training, work placement, mentoring) is based on general programme descriptions, not the form.
‘A reply within 4 to 6 weeks’ is an estimate.
